source_jobs.title: Senior Analytics Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Relay is fundamentally reshaping how goods move in an online era. Backed by Europe’s largest-ever logistics Series A ($35M), led by deep-tech investors Plural (whose portfolio spans fusion energy and space exploration), Relay is scaling faster than 99.98% of venture-backed startups. We're assembling the most talent-dense team the logistics industry has ever seen

Relay’s Mission is to free commerce from friction. Today, high delivery costs act as a hidden tax on e-commerce, quietly shaping what can be sold online and limiting who can participate. We envision a world where more goods move more freely between more people, making the online shopping experience seamless and accessible to everyone.



THE TEAM
• ~110 people, more than half in engineering, product and data
• 45+ advanced degrees across computer science, mathematics and operations research
• Thousands of data points captured, calculated, analysed and predicted for every single parcel we handle
• An intellectually vibrant culture of first‑principles thinking, tight feedback loops and relentless experimentation



The Opportunity

At Relay, we rely on data to guide how every parcel moves through our network — from when it enters the system to when it's delivered at the doorstep. Our data infrastructure is the backbone that powers everything from real-time operational decisions to strategic business insights to networks of autonomous AI agents.

We're looking for a Senior Analytics Engineer to sit within our Last Mile squad; our biggest and most data rich squad. Your role will help contribute to the success of the key agentic workflows that power our Last Mile marketplace.

You'll be joining at an exciting moment as we grow rapidly and expand our network across the UK.




Who Will Thrive in this role?

 - Design core data models - Partner with engineers and business leaders within the Last Mile squad to design and build foundational data models that power analytics, data science and AI workflows

 - Build the foundations for our key AI systems - Our Last Mile marketplace is becoming increasingly agentic, you will be a key owner of the inputs that power these systems

 - Drive data quality - Implement testing, monitoring, and alerting to ensure our data is accurate, timely, and trustworthy

 - Collaborate - Work with team members across the business to understand their data needs and deliver solutions

 - Shape our practices — Help define and evolve our analytics engineering processes and standards as we scale



What We're Looking For

 - 5+ years of experience as an analytics engineer, data engineer, data analyst or similar role

 - Extensive experience with SQL, particularly with BigQuery and PostgreSQL

 - Demonstrated experience with dbt, with a track record of building and maintaining efficient, scalable, and reliable DAGs

 - Python proficiency for data manipulation, scripting, and automation

 - Experience with data visualisation tools such as Tableau, Looker, or similar

 - Experience in effective communication, particularly in gathering requirements and guiding non-technical stakeholders to the right data solutions

 - Familiarity with using AI tools to accelerate your workflow



Nice to Have:

 - Experience with orchestration tools like Dagster, Airflow, or similar

 - Experience building real-time or near-real-time data pipelines

 - Familiarity with machine learning workflows and feature engineering

 - Experience embedded in teams building customer facing products

 - Previous experience of learning and modelling an unfamiliar operational domain

 - A desire to see the real world impact of what you work on!



Compensation, Benefits & Workplace

 - Generous equity, richer than 99% of European startups, with annual top-ups to share Relay’s success.

 - Private health & dental coverage, so comprehensive you’d need to be a partner at a Magic Circle law firm to match it.

 - 25 days of holidays

 - Enhanced parental leave.

 - Hardware of your choice.

 - Ext

Work Alongside Industry Leaders

Diego Protas – Director of Engineering

Diego, an expert in distributed systems and hardware architecture, merging physical computing with enterprise-scale infrastructure. Previously directing teams of 170+ engineers at Mercado Libre and orchestrating large-scale ML-based inference at Meta. At Relay, Diego’s infectious enthusiasm and hands-on leadership are redefining the boundaries of speed and reliability.

source_jobs.description:

We're assembling the most talent-dense team the logistics industry has ever seen

Relay’s Mission is to free commerce from friction. Today, high delivery costs act as a hidden tax on e-commerce, quietly shaping what can be sold online and limiting who can participate. We envision a world where more goods move more freely between more people, making the online shopping experience seamless and accessible to everyone.



THE TEAM
• ~110 people, more than half in engineering, product and data
• 45+ advanced degrees across computer science, mathematics and operations research
• Thousands of data points captured, calculated, analysed and predicted for every single parcel we handle
• An intellectually vibrant culture of first‑principles thinking, tight feedback loops and relentless experimentation



The Opportunity



Relay's network runs on forecasts. How many parcels arrive tomorrow. Which ones to sort tonight. How many shifts to release. When those forecasts are wrong, the cost shows up in CPP: too few shifts and you pay surge premiums, too many and you waste capacity. Behind each forecast is a model, and those models need someone watching them, catching drift, and surfacing problems before they hit operations.

As Lead Data Analyst for the Network squad, you will own the analytical oversight across Relay's forecasting systems. That means tracking model performance across demand forecasting, shift planning, dimensions, and BPP inclusion. When a model starts missing, you catch it. When forecast error costs money, you quantify it. When stakeholders say "the forecast was wrong," you investigate and build the case for what to fix.

This is not a model-building role. The Network squad's Data Scientists build and ship the forecasting models. Your job is to ensure those models keep working, to set analytical standards for the squad, and to be the first line of defence when something breaks. You will work closely with Sortation, Middle Mile, Last Mile, Routing, and Finance to understand where forecasts are working and where they're not.

Relay operates a centralised data team of around 30 data engineers, analysts, and data scientists, with analysts embedded into squads across the business. You will sit in the Network squad, reporting to the Squad Lead. Depending on your experience, this role also offers line management opportunities.



What You'll Do

 - Track forecast accuracy across all Network models: demand forecasts, shift planning forecasts, dimensions model, BPP inclusion models

 - Surface model drift and performance issues before they impact operations - you're the early warning system

 - Diagnose forecast errors by client, geography, day of week, and horizon, and quantify the CPP impact

 - Build dashboards that make model performance transparent to the squad and stakeholders

 - Set analytical direction for the Network squad: define what metrics matter, where to invest analytical effort

 - Work with Data Scientists to build the business case for model improvements, whether that's new input signals, data quality fixes, or client-specific treatment

 - Monitor how capacity matches demand across Sortation, Middle Mile, Last Mile, and track shift release effectiveness

 - Translate model performance into business impact that stakeholders can act on



Who Will Thrive in This Role?

 - You catch problems before they become crises - you're vigilant about when models start behaving unexpectedly

 - You're obsessive about data quality and forecast accuracy - you hold models accountable for their predictions

 - You're fluent in SQL and experienced with dbt/BigQuery - you can build and maintain analytical models

 - You've built dashboards that are opera

• ~110 people, more than half in engineering, product and data
• 45+ advanced degrees across computer science, mathematics and operations research
• Thousands of data points captured, calculated, analysed and predicted for every single parcel we handle
• An intellectually vibrant culture of first‑principles thinking, tight feedback loops and relentless experimentation



The Opportunity

Relay’s Hybrid Sort Centres are where parcels enter the network, get sorted into routes, and flow out to delivery. Sortation is operationally complex: warehouse operatives, intake scanners, supervisors, and robots all working together to process thousands of parcels daily. First Mile is how those parcels arrive - collections from retailers, with all the variability that brings. Together, these domains sit at the start of the network and set the pace for everything downstream.

As a Data Analyst in the Sortation & First Mile squad, you will own the analytics for both domains. The work is broad: cost investigations, workforce planning, capacity analysis, performance tracking, and operational reporting. Your analysis won’t sit in a dashboard waiting to be discovered - it will shape how shifts are scheduled, how volumes are managed, and where the operation focuses its improvement efforts.

Relay operates a centralised data team of around 30 data engineers, analysts, and data scientists, with analysts embedded into squads across the business. You will sit in the Sortation & First Mile squad, but report into the centralised data team. You’ll work closely with operations, commercial, and finance to turn data into decisions that affect how the network runs every day.



What You’ll Do

 - Investigate cost per parcel: where are we spending too much, where are savings available, and what’s driving the variance

 - Track operative performance across roles: throughput, accuracy, shift utilisation, and the balance between direct and agency labour

 - Monitor robot efficiency and capacity: what volumes can they sustain, where are the bottlenecks, how do they compare to manual processing

 - Build models to predict shift requirements and automate workforce planning, prioritising top-performing operatives

 - Analyse injection volumes and manifest accuracy: how much are we actually sorting vs. what’s forecast, and where are the gaps

 - Define KPIs and build dashboards that make sortation and first mile performance transparent to operations and leadership

 - Work with commercial teams to set volume limits and injection timing that balance growth with operational stability



Who Will Thrive in This Role?

 - You want to understand how an operation actually works, not just analyse it from a distance

 - You’re excited by cost problems - finding where money is leaking and building the case for how to fix it

 - You can navigate messy operational data and turn it into metrics people trust

 - You’re comfortable working with operations teams and embedding your analysis into their daily workflows

 - You’re fluent in SQL and experienced with BI tools, with strong problem-solving instincts

 - You have at least 3 years experience, ideally with some exposure to logistics

 - You care about impact, not just insight - you measure success by whether the operation run

The Opportunity

Relay is building the UK's most intelligent delivery network. Every parcel that moves through our system makes the network smarter, and we hold ourselves to a high bar on the metrics that prove it: on-time attempts, on-time delivery, did-not-receive rates, lost-in-transit rates, unit economics, and client profitability. Our clients hold us to that same bar, and our commercial org is where those conversations happen. Behind every strategic account is a rich set of numbers that need to be read, interpreted, and turned into a story. This role owns that.

As Commercial Analytics Programme Lead, you own the commercial analytics programme across our strategic accounts. This role is critical in keeping our commercial metrics moving in the right direction (performance and quality, profitability, growth), and communicating that improvement to clients in a way that meets their priorities. AMs and their clients bring you the things that matter most. You take those needs, find the root cause across our network and commercial ops, and drive the right internal teams to fix them. You're the connective tissue between what clients are asking for and what Relay's squads are building, and you stay across both sides until what we're saying externally matches what we're delivering internally.

You run multiple commercial analytics programmes in parallel across the full strategic account base: performance and quality, profitability, and growth. Each has its own cadence, KPIs, and reporting standards, and you own all of them. When an AM brings a live problem (a DNR spike, a margin slip, a pipeline risk), you triage it across the account base, diagnose where in the value chain the root cause sits, and commission the right internal teams to fix it. You sequence their work so the fix lands in time for the client conversation. You manage a Commercial Analyst as a direct report, with the other analytical resources across the business (growth and expansion, quality, finance) on dotted lines in so you can pull together the full commercial picture.

Relay has best in class operational teams, account managers, and data capabilities. Our squads build and deliver. This role keeps them pointed at the commercial problems that matter, challenges their output, and holds them to the dates we've committed externally. You coordinate across quality, finance, growth and expansion, and the operational pillars (first mile, sort, middle mile / partner services, last mile), and you're creative about how you pull resource together when no single squad owns the problem. The job has two sides that run together: moving commercial metrics through the internal delivery, and communicating that progress to clients in a way that meets their priorities. You own both. Relay is investing heavily in quality and observability systems that will increasingly tell us exactly where we're winning and losing

• ~110 people, more than half in engineering, product and data
• 45+ advanced degrees across computer science, mathematics and operations research
• Thousands of data points captured, calculated, analysed and predicted for every single parcel we handle
• An intellectually vibrant culture of first‑principles thinking, tight feedback loops and relentless experimentation



The Opportunity

Relay's network is scaling fast. The decisions that shape that growth - where to expand, how to price, where to invest - rest on models that simulate how the network behaves and how it evolves as density, geography, and operating models change. We have an MVP tool for this today; we need someone to help productionise it and build the next generation of strategic models on top of it.

You'll be a core contributor to Relay's digital twin - a network simulator that captures our unit economics end-to-end, from first-mile collection through sortation, middle-mile and last-mile. It's already in use by finance, but it's a living system: every operating-model change, new service type, or commercial scenario means upgrading a component, adding a model, or building a new view. Your job is to help keep it accurate and to extend it where the business asks new questions.



Your remit splits roughly equally between two halves:

1. Evolving the digital twin. You understand each cost component deeply, you help identify where the current models are wrong or incomplete, and you upgrade them systematically.

 - When a new first-mile operating model is rolled out, you upgrade that engine.

 - When a new service type changes sortation cost, you understand it at a granular level and model it properly.

 - You add new flows, surface new metrics, and harden the parts that matter most for the decisions being made.

2. Building new strategic models and forecasts. Volume forecasts, predictive models, new scenario tooling - work that sits inside or alongside the twin.

 - You help decide what should live in the twin and what should stand alone.

 - You pick the right tool for the question - classical statistical modelling, financial modelling, simulation, or ML where it earns its place.



Your primary partner is the finance team - Strategic Finance, Commercial Finance, FP&A. You translate their questions into modelling problems and give them tools that let them explore pricing, margin and projection scenarios dynamically rather than waiting for analysis to be rebuilt manually. You contribute to the roadmap - taking in their needs, helping prioritise what to build, and shipping it.

You're part of a data org of around 30 engineers, analysts, and data scientists, but embedded in the finance squad. A dedicated finance analyst within the squad partners with you directly - they build the reporting and visibility layer on top of your models and feed prioritisation back to you. You'll work alongside more senior data scientists who own the broader direction of the twin.



Who will thrive in this role

 - You're a strong modeller who thinks in systems. You naturally break complex problems into components, understand the assumptions behind each one, and know when those assumptions need revisiting.

Relay’s Mission is to free commerce from friction. Today, high delivery costs act as a hidden tax on e-commerce, quietly shaping what can be sold online and limiting who can participate. We envision a world where more goods move more freely between more people, making the online shopping experience seamless and accessible to everyone.

• ~110 people, more than half in engineering, product and data
• 45+ advanced degrees across computer science, mathematics and operations research
• Thousands of data points captured, calculated, analysed and predicted for every single parcel we handle
• An intellectually vibrant culture of first‑principles thinking, tight feedback loops and relentless experimentation



Overview

Relay's Pitstop network is the convenience-store backbone of the operation. Customers drop off and collect parcels at these locations, Middle Mile drivers hand off and pick up, and the whole network depends on independent retailers behaving in ways that make Relay work. They aren't Relay staff. We don't control their attention, their turnover, or what else is happening behind their counter. Every behaviour we need has to be produced through systems, incentives, and the app.

The Pitstop squad's job is to build those systems. Today the network runs at around 170k parcels a day, propped up by a small operations team and twelve LiveOps FTE absorbing the day-to-day breakage. The squad's mission is to take the network from 170k to 2M parcels a day without LiveOps scaling linearly with it. That means systematising launch readiness, pitstop performance, pricing structure, and retention so the same number of operators can support a network twelve times the size.

As a Data Analyst in the Pitstop squad, you will own the analytics that make all of this measurable. Are launches hitting day-1 quality? Is handover speed improving where new in-app behaviour nudges have rolled out? Which pitstops are heading for churn and why? What's actually driving LiveOps ticket load, and which interventions are removing it? You'll build the monitoring layer the squad relies on, run the cost and performance investigations that shape what gets built next, and become the squad's source of truth on what's working.

Relay operates a centralised data team of around 30 data engineers, analysts, and data scientists, with analysts embedded into squads across the business. You will sit in the Pitstop squad, working day-to-day with operations, sales, engineering, design, and research. You will report into the centralised data team and be line managed by the Lead Data Analyst for Middle Mile, Pitstops & Sortation.



What You'll Do

 - Own the Pitstop squad's five outcome metrics: Launch Velocity, Handover Speed, Handover Accuracy, Pitstop Retention, and Indirect Cost per Parcel. Build the monitoring around them, investigate movement, and translate the numbers into next actions for the squad.

 - Run the analytics behind pitstop performance and pricing: which behaviours move handover speed, how commercial tiers and clawbacks are landing, which pitstops are systematically under- or over-performing, and what the in-app nudges are actually changing on the counter.

 - Quantify the impact of squad initiatives in cost terms: how much LiveOps load did this self-serve flow remove, how much Middle Mile driver time did this handover prompt unlock, and what is the CPP impact of retention work on mature pitstops.

 - Build launch readiness analytics: cohort ramp curves, time-to-maturity, where new pitstops break in week one, and how the launch playbook should evolve as volume grows.

THE TEAM
• ~110 people, more than half in engineering, product and data
• 45+ advanced degrees across computer science, mathematics and operations research
• Thousands of data points captured, calculated, analysed and predicted for every single parcel we handle
• An intellectually vibrant culture of first‑principles thinking, tight feedback loops and relentless experimentation



The Opportunity

Relay’s Last Mile network runs on couriers - tens of thousands of them, doing real work in the physical world every day. Whether each parcel gets delivered successfully depends on the decisions and behaviours of the courier on route. We're hiring for a Senior Data Analyst to own how Relay measures and improves courier excellence: how we shape what happens on the doorstep, and how we manage performance before and after that interaction.



This role works with the whole of the Last Mile team and partners closely with data scientists, engineers and operators across the squad. There's a v1 of performance management already running; you'll own its evolution into something materially better.



Last Mile

Handover Optimisation: Every handover is a trade-off between competing outcomes - speed, quality, and risk. Moving any one of them can have knock-on effects on the others, and finding the right balance is one of the most interesting analytical problems in the network. Your job is to make those trade-offs visible, model them, and find the levers that move the network in the right direction. You'll run the experiments, design the measurement, and quantify what actually works.

Performance Management: Relay already has a framework for measuring courier performance, and it feeds into how work is distributed. The job now is to ensure it drives fleet quality - defining the right behaviours to measure, evaluating the interventions designed to help couriers shift those behaviours, and closing the loop so the framework continuously improves. You'll partner with product, ops and engineering across this work, with the analytics owned by you.

Courier-facing analytics: Couriers are the end users of much of this work. You'll think about how performance and rewards are made transparent to them, and you'll run experiments to evaluate whether interventions are actually working for couriers and for Relay.



Who Will Thrive in This Role

 - You're a strong analyst who thinks in systems. You break complex problems into components and know where the real opportunity lies.

 - You are successful working with high autonomy and are excited to operate in a Product/Programme Manager like capacity, defining the roadmap as well as executing against it.

 - You care about solving the problem more than which techniques you use.

 - You love working with smart, motivated people.

 - You're fluent in SQL.

 - You're comfortable across the analytical spectrum - designing and reading experiments, building risk scores and segmentation, and shipping descriptive analysis that drives a decision the same week.

 - You communicate inclusively with non-technical team members.

 - You love seeing the impact of your work play out in a daily operation.

 - Bonus: You have experience working on performance management, marketplace quality, operational analytics or another relevant domain.

COMPANY MISSION

In the future, almost everything we consume will simply materialise on our doorsteps – what we call “e-commerce” today will simply be “commerce” tomorrow. But if we continue on today’s trajectory, the growth of e-commerce risks damaging the environment, alienating our communities, and straining the bottom-line for small businesses.



Relay is an e-commerce-native logistics network. We are built from the ground up for environmental, social, and economic sustainability. By building from the ground up we are able to entirely rethink both the middle and last mile enabling us to reduce the number of miles driven to deliver each parcel, lower carbon emissions, and lower costs, all while channelling funds to community members.

About the role

Relay's network runs on forecasts. Every shift released in sortation, every middle-mile van dispatched, every last-mile route planned, every expansion decision made - all downstream of models that predict how parcels move through our system. When those models are right, the network runs efficiently and cost per parcel drops. When they drift, the cost compounds across every stage of the operation. The Network squad builds and maintains the forecasting engine that powers all of it.

As a Senior Data Scientist in the Network squad, you will lead a core domain within that engine, working alongside other Data Scientists and Analysts who each bring different expertise. The scope spans demand forecasting, expansion modelling, parcel intelligence, and sortation predictions - and the specific domain you take on will depend on your strengths and what the squad needs most. What's common across all of it: you will build and maintain models that directly drive operational decisions for multiple teams across Relay, every day.

This means different things depending on the domain. It might mean building the expansion models that determine where Relay grows next: which outcodes to enter, how volumes ramp in new areas, and how the pitstop network should evolve. It might mean working on the dimensions model that predicts parcel weights and sizes before they arrive, feeding route planning and vehicle loading decisions. It might mean extending the demand forecast horizon from 7 days toward 30, giving downstream teams more lead time to plan. Or it might mean improving the predictions that determine what volume is available to sort on a given night, and how that volume should be allocated across sort centres.

Relay operates a centralised data team of around 30 Data Engineers, Analysts, and Data Scientists, with specialists embedded into squads across the business. You will sit in the Network squad alongside other Data Scientists and Analysts, reporting into the centralised data team. The squad is growing, and you will have significant influence over its technical direction and the modelling approaches it adopts. You will be supported by Analysts who build the monitoring and reporting layer, and a squad lead who sets strategic direction.

 - Build and maintain forecasting models within your domain - from initial exploration through to validation and production deployment

source_jobs.description:
Relay is fundamentally reshaping how goods move in an online era. Backed by Europe’s largest-ever logistics Series A ($35M), led by deep-tech investors Plural (whose portfolio spans fusion energy and space exploration), Relay is scaling faster than 99.98% of venture-backed startups. We're assembling the most talent-dense team the logistics industry has ever seen

Relay’s Mission is to free commerce from friction. Today, high delivery costs act as a hidden tax on e-commerce, quietly shaping what can be sold online and limiting who can participate. We envision a world where more goods move more freely between more people, making the online shopping experience seamless and accessible to everyone.



THE TEAM
• ~110 people, more than half in engineering, product and data
• 45+ advanced degrees across computer science, mathematics and operations research
• Thousands of data points captured, calculated, analysed and predicted for every single parcel we handle
• An intellectually vibrant culture of first‑principles thinking, tight feedback loops and relentless experimentation

The Opportunity

Please note: We offer Day and Night Shifts

As the Shift Manager at Relay, you can expect to have the following responsibilities 

 - Overseeing sortation operations during your shift to ensure targets are met

 - Supporting with recruitment, onboarding, and training of site operatives

 - Leading, motivating, and coaching your team to high performance

 - Ensuring health & safety standards are upheld and continuously improved

 - Identifying and acting on opportunities to drive efficiency and service quality

 - Collaborating with stakeholders to support the wider success of the site
   

Who Will Thrive in this role?

 - Previous experience in a fast-paced operational or warehouse environment

 - A proactive leader with strong communication and team-building skills

 - Able to think on your feet, solve problems, and make sound decisions under pressure

 - Comfortable using data to track performance and make improvements

 - A hands-on, positive attitude and a willingness to roll up your sleeves
   
   

Fast and Focused Hiring Process

 1. Talent Acquisition Interview - 30 min

 2. Hiring Manager Interview - 90 mins

 3. Final Interview - 1 hour

 4. Decision and offer within 48 hours. Our process mirrors our pace of work.



Compensation, Benefits & Workplace

 - Generous equity, richer than 99% of European startups, with annual top-ups to share Relay’s success.

Who Thrives at Relay?

 - Aim with Precision: You define problems clearly and measure your impact meticulously.

 - 1% Better Every Day: You believe that small, consistent improvements lead to exponential growth. You move quickly, deliver results, and learn from every experience.
